title: Plug it in and Play: Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:PnP-RotS-Vader.jpg]] |title=''Plug it in and Play:
Star Wars Episode III:
Revenge of the Sith'' |developer=[[Jakks TV Games]] |publisher=[[Lucasfilm]] |release date=[[February 25]], [[2005]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.jakkstvgames.com/starwars.html|text=Star Wars Episode 3|work=[https://web.archive.org/web/20080203111102/http://www.jakkstvgames.com JAKKS TV Games]|archivedate=20080207013003}} |closed= |upc= |engine= |genre=Plug In, Action, Adventure |modes= |ratings= |platforms= |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director= |producer= |designer= |programmer= |artist= |writer= |composer= |timeline=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |prev= |next= }} '''''Plug it in and Play: Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith''''' is a [[Star Wars video games|video game]] released in [[February]] of [[2005]]. It is one of the series of plug'n'play consoles made by Jakks TV Games. It is available in four different versions: [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] Controller (the most common), [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] Controller, [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] Controller, and the General [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] Controller (most rare). ==Gameplay== The game has five missions as two characters. The first mission is titled "[[Droid/Legends|Droid]] Invasion". The player plays as [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] as he fights [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. Playable places in this mission are [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], and [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]]. The second mission is titled "[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] Attack" and recreates the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]]. The third mission is called "[[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] Onslaught", in which the goal is to rescue [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] from the [[Invisible Hand/Legends|''Invisible Hand'']]. The fourth mission is titled "Gunship Battle". The objective is to pick up troops from the battlefield and manage to get four [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] into the enemy base(s) without being shot down four times. Locations are [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] and Geonosis. The fifth mission is "[[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] Chase", in which the player as Obi-Wan Kenobi atop [[Boga/Legends|Boga]] chases General Grievous through the streets of Utapau while battling enemies on speeder bikes. ===Expansions=== The Star Wars edition of the Jakks Pacific Game Key adds an extra two missions to the gaming device and there is a slot for this Game Key on all [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], and [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] controllers. New games from this expansion cartridge include "Turret Defense", where on either [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]], or [[Mygeeto/Legends|Mygeeto]], you have to use a turret to prevent any Separatist droids from landing on the planet surface, and "Yoda's Escape", in which Yoda has to escape from Kashyyyk while fighting clone troopers along the way. ===Controllers=== File:PnP-RotS-Vader.jpg|Darth Vader File:PnP-RotS-Grievous.jpg|Grievous File:PnP-RotS-R2.jpg|R2-D2 ==Continuity== The LAAT's secondary weapon is a proton bomb, even though no other source has listed
